concept.name: justify-self
concept.explain:
The 'justify-self' property is used to align a grid item within its grid cell in the inline direction.
This property can have one of the following values: auto (default), normal, stretch, start, left, center, end, right.
Note: For this property to have any alignment effect, the grid item needs available space around itself in the inline direction.
Example: justify-self: start;

Use this property to adjust the horizontal alignment of the car without changing its grid area.

ui.prompt: Keep the car in the same 3×2 area, then align it to the right edge using justify-self.
hints: Do not change grid-column or grid-row.

entities.baseCss: background: rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.22); outline: 2px dashed rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.8); border-radius: 10px; min-width: 24px; justify-self: end;

concept.name: align-self
concept.explain:
The 'align-self' property is used to align a grid item within its grid cell in the block direction.
This property can have one of the following values: auto (default), normal, stretch, start, end, center.
Note: For this property to have any alignment effect, the grid item needs available space around itself in the block direction.
Example: align-self: left;

Use this property to adjust the vertical alignment of the car without changing its grid area.

ui.prompt: Keep the car in the same 2×3 area, then align it to the bottom edge using align-self.
hints: Do not change grid-column or grid-row.

entities.baseCss: background: rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.22); outline: 2px dashed rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.8); border-radius: 10px; min-height: 24px; align-self: end;

concept.name: place-self
concept.explain:
The 'place-self' property is a shorthand for 'align-self' and 'justify-self'.
This property can have one of the following values: auto (default), normal, stretch, start, end, center.
Note: For this property to have any alignment effect, the grid item needs available space around itself in both the inline and block directions.
Example: place-self: start;

Use this property to adjust both horizontal and vertical alignment of the car without changing its grid area.

ui.prompt: Match the target by centering the car inside the same 3×3 area using place-self.
hints: Do not change grid-column or grid-row.

entities.baseCss: background: rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.22); outline: 2px dashed rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.8); border-radius: 10px; min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; place-self: center;
